blocks.body: Degrees of comparison show how qualities relate to one another. There are three forms: the basic form, the comparative, and the superlative.

{ "title": "7. Quick summary", "items": [ "Short words (1-2 syllables) usually take -er / -est: fast → faster → the fastest.", "Long words (3+ syllables) usually use more / most: interesting → more interesting.", "Irregular forms include good/better/best, bad/worse/worst, far/farther/farthest.", "Intensifiers include much/way/a lot + comparative and by far + superlative.", "Equality uses as + adjective + as; inequality uses not as/so ... as.", "Avoid double markers and do not forget the before superlatives." ] }
